title: Real Estate Deals in Billings MT: A Complete Investor's Guide for 2024
excerpt: Billings, Montana is one of the most underrated markets for real estate investors, offering affordable entry points, strong rental demand, and a growing economy. This guide breaks down the best neighborhoods, deal types, and strategies for finding profitable real estate deals in Billings MT.
author: AIWholesail Team
tags: real estate deals in Billings MT
metaDescription: Find profitable real estate deals in Billings MT. Explore top neighborhoods, market trends, wholesale, flip & rental strategies for 2024 investors.
metaKeywords: real estate deals in Billings MT, Billings Montana real estate investing, wholesale real estate Billings, investment properties Billings MT, Billings MT rental market, fix and flip Billings Montana, off-market deals Billings MT

sections.content: Billings, Montana doesn't get nearly the attention it deserves from real estate investors. While bigger markets like Denver and Phoenix grab all the headlines, Billings quietly offers something many investors can't find elsewhere: affordable home prices, steady population growth, and strong rental demand driven by energy, healthcare, and agriculture industries. Whether you're a seasoned flipper, a buy-and-hold landlord, or a wholesaler looking for your next assignment fee, real estate deals in Billings MT are absolutely worth your attention in 2024. If you're just getting started, check out our [Real Estate Investing for Beginners](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/real-estate-investing-for-beginners) guide before diving in.
Billings MT Market Overview: Why Investors Are Taking Notice

Billings is Montana's largest city with a population of around 120,000. It serves as the economic hub of the entire region, anchored by two major hospital systems, a thriving oil and gas sector, and a robust agricultural economy. Unlike many mid-sized cities that peaked and stalled, Billings has shown consistent population growth averaging around 1-2% annually over the past decade. The unemployment rate consistently tracks below the national average, which is a key indicator of a healthy rental market. For investors, this translates to tenants who can pay rent reliably and a steady stream of buyers when it's time to sell.
Average Home Prices in Billings MT

As of 2024, the median home price in Billings sits around $325,000 to $350,000, which is significantly lower than comparable-sized cities in the Mountain West. Entry-level single-family homes in distressed or older neighborhoods can still be found in the $150,000 to $220,000 range, making this market genuinely accessible for investors working with smaller budgets. Duplexes and small multifamily properties typically run between $280,000 and $450,000 depending on condition and location. Average rents for a 3-bedroom single-family home range from $1,400 to $1,900 per month, which creates solid cash-on-cash return potential, especially if you're buying below market value. You can explore more local data on our dedicated [Billings MT market page](https://aiwholesail.com/markets/billings-mt).
Best Neighborhoods for Real Estate Investors in Billings MT
Not every zip code in Billings will deliver the same results. Knowing where to focus your search is half the battle when hunting for deals.

items: Downtown & South Side: Older housing stock, motivated sellers, and strong rental demand from healthcare workers and young professionals. Great for BRRRR and flip strategies. Expect to find homes built in the 1950s–1980s needing cosmetic to moderate rehab work.

Poly Drive & Heights Area: A working-class neighborhood with consistent rental demand. Lower price points make it ideal for buy-and-hold investors. Cash flow is easier to achieve here than in more expensive zip codes.
Rimrock Area: More established and slightly higher price points, but strong appreciation potential. Better suited for fix-and-flip investors looking for a solid resale market.
West End: A growing suburb with newer construction and higher owner-occupant demand. Less wholesale opportunity but excellent for retail flips targeting move-in-ready buyers.
Lockwood (unincorporated): Just east of Billings, Lockwood offers lower property taxes and is popular with families. Rental demand is solid and competition from other investors is lower.

sections.content: Pro Tip: Drive the South Side and Heights neighborhoods first if you're visiting Billings in person. You'll spot the most distressed properties there, and these ZIP codes consistently produce the best wholesale and flip margins in the market.
Types of Real Estate Deals Available in Billings MT
Wholesale Deals

Wholesaling is very viable in Billings because the investor community is active but not oversaturated. A typical wholesale deal might involve finding a distressed single-family home with an ARV of $250,000, getting it under contract at $140,000, and assigning the contract to a local flipper for a $10,000 to $20,000 assignment fee. The key is building relationships with local real estate attorneys and title companies who understand assignment contracts, since Montana has specific requirements around real estate contracts. If you're new to the strategy, read our [Wholesaling Real Estate for Beginners](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/wholesaling-real-estate-for-beginners) guide to understand the mechanics before you start making offers.
Fix and Flip Deals

Billings is a healthy fix-and-flip market in 2024. Renovation costs run slightly higher than the national average due to Montana's contractor shortage and material transportation costs — budget $25 to $45 per square foot for light to moderate rehabs. The good news is that after-repair values have held steady, and days on market for renovated homes remains relatively low at 20 to 40 days. Use our free [ARV Calculator](https://aiwholesail.com/tools/arv-calculator) to quickly estimate your after-repair value before making an offer, so you never overpay on a deal.
Buy-and-Hold Rental Properties

The rental market in Billings is one of its strongest selling points. Vacancy rates hover around 4 to 5%, which is considered very tight by industry standards. The combination of steady job growth, a large healthcare workforce, and a lack of new rental supply keeps demand high. Single-family rentals and small multifamily properties (2–4 units) offer the best risk-adjusted returns. A duplex purchased at $300,000 renting for $900 per unit can generate meaningful cash flow, especially with a 20 to 25% down payment on a conventional loan.
Local Market Trends to Watch in 2024

items: Inventory remains tight: Active listings are below historical averages, which keeps prices stable and makes off-market outreach more valuable than ever.
Interest rate impact: Higher rates have pushed some buyer demand into the rental market, benefiting landlords but also cooling retail flip exit times slightly.
Energy sector influence: Billings benefits from Bakken oil activity in neighboring North Dakota. Any uptick in oil prices tends to tighten the rental market further as energy workers relocate.
Healthcare expansion: Both Billings Clinic and St. Vincent Healthcare continue to expand, bringing stable, well-paid employees who need housing.
Out-of-state migration: Montana as a whole has seen increased in-migration from California, Washington, and Oregon. This is slowly pushing up prices in Billings, which benefits investors who buy now.

sections.content: "The best real estate markets aren't always the ones everyone is talking about — they're the ones with steady jobs, affordable housing, and growing populations that haven't yet been discovered by institutional money." — Common wisdom among successful regional real estate investors
How to Find Off-Market Real Estate Deals in Billings MT

On-market deals in Billings move quickly, and you'll often be competing with local buyers who know the market well. The real money is in [off-market real estate deals](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/off-market-real-estate-deals), where you can negotiate directly with motivated sellers before a property hits the MLS. Effective strategies in Billings include direct mail campaigns targeting absentee owners and tax-delinquent properties, driving for dollars in the South Side and Heights neighborhoods, building relationships with local probate attorneys, and networking with the Billings Association of Realtors and local REIA groups. Consistent outreach over 90 to 180 days typically produces the best results in this market.
Practical Tips for Out-of-State Investors

Investing remotely in Billings is absolutely doable, but it requires the right team on the ground. Before you make your first offer, you need a reliable property manager, a contractor you trust, and a local real estate attorney familiar with Montana law. Montana is an attorney-closing state, meaning an attorney must handle closings rather than a title company in many cases — know this before you structure your first deal. Always [analyze your deals thoroughly](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/how-to-analyze-real-estate-deals) using real local comps, not just Zestimate estimates, which can be inaccurate in smaller markets like Billings.

items: Visit the market at least once before buying: A 2-day trip to walk neighborhoods and meet your team is worth more than hours of online research.
Use a local property manager from day one: Self-managing from out of state in a market you don't know is a recipe for expensive mistakes.
Vet contractors carefully: Montana has a contractor shortage. Get three bids, check references, and never pay more than 10% upfront on any job.
Run your numbers conservatively: Use 10% for vacancy, 10% for maintenance, and 8 to 10% for property management in your cash flow analysis.
Build a local network: Join the Montana Real Estate Investors Association and connect with active Billings wholesalers and agents on BiggerPockets.

sections.content: Pro Tip: Before making any offer in Billings, run your numbers through our free [Wholesale Deal Calculator](https://aiwholesail.com/tools/wholesale-deal-calculator) to instantly see your maximum allowable offer, estimated profit, and whether the deal actually pencils out. It takes less than 2 minutes and can save you from a costly mistake.
Is Billings MT the Right Market for You?

Billings isn't a flashy market, and that's exactly why it works. It offers the fundamentals that matter most to long-term investors: affordability, employment diversity, population growth, and strong rental demand. Whether you're looking to wholesale your first deal, flip a dated ranch house, or build a cash-flowing rental portfolio, real estate deals in Billings MT offer a realistic path to profit in 2024 without the extreme competition and sky-high prices you'll find in more glamorous markets. The window of opportunity in Billings is still open — but as out-of-state migration continues and more investors discover the market, that window will narrow.
